claims.found: internal_type.SpeechToTextPacket{ ContextID: ctxID, Script: alternative.Transcript, Confidence: alternative.Confidence, Language: lang, Interim: false } emitted inside the mr.IsFinal branch at line 91-97

claims.claim: Together: Quality = Word Error Rate, SOTA ~6% WER on open benchmarks, errors unrecoverable - LLM and TTS carry mistake forward (together-talk:15)
claims.found: line 15: 'Quality = Word Error Rate. SOTA ~6% WER on open benchmarks. Errors are unrecoverable — LLM and TTS carry the mistake forward.'

claims.claim: latency = time to complete transcript after user stops, P90 ~100ms (together-talk:16)
claims.found: line 16: 'Latency = time to complete transcript after the user stops speaking. Together runs models at P90 ~100ms.'

claims.found: line 816 (two-column wrapped): '...validates that domain-specific terminology accuracy and environmental robustness matter more than raw WER in production voice-led sales workflows.'

claims.claim: No clone attempts post-hoc transcript correction; final transcript handed verbatim

summary: All six checkable assertions verified against ground truth. Code_ref stt_callback.go:91 genuinely emits SpeechToTextPacket with alternative.Transcript + Confidence in the IsFinal branch. Both together-talk figures (6% WER, P90 ~100ms) are exact quotes at lines 15-16. Deepgram terminology-vs-WER quote is verbatim at line 816. No WRONG or MISCITED claims.
